question.text: What is the minimum net tangible asset requirement for a company making an IPO (if meeting profitability route)?

answer.text: At least Rs. 3 crores in each of the preceding three full years
At least Rs. 10 crores in each of the preceding three full years
At least Rs. 1 crore in each of the preceding three full years
At least Rs. 5 crores in each of the preceding three full years

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under SEBI ICDR Regulations, an issuer may make an IPO if it has net tangible assets of at least Rs. 3 crores in each of the preceding three full years, of which not more than 50% are held in monetary assets (with exceptions). **The Trap:** Rs. 1 crore (option c) is the minimum net worth requirement, not net tangible assets. Rs. 3 crore for net tangible assets and Rs. 1 crore for net worth are different eligibility criteria. **Cross-Exam Context:** Additional requirements: average operating profit of Rs. 15 crores over 3 years, and net worth of Rs. 1 crore per year for 3 years. Companies not meeting these can still issue via book building with 75% QIB allotment. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Profitability route: Rs. 3 cr net tangible assets + Rs. 15 cr avg operating profit + Rs. 1 cr net worth. All for 3 preceding years.
